steps.title: Use factored form to display the zeros
steps.content: A form that displays the zeros should look like $(x-r)(x-s)$, where the zeros are $r$ and $s$. For $x^2-42x+80$, the two factor constants must multiply to $80$ and add to $-42$.

steps.title: Scan the choices for 2 and 40 as visible literals
steps.content:
Factoring by inspection gives the same result: two numbers that multiply to $80$ and add to $-42$ are $-2$ and $-40$, so
$$f(x)=(x-2)(x-40)$$
That form displays the zeros $x=2$ and $x=40$ as constants, which matches choice B.
